content:
- Completed task 03 end to end: committed parser/test/task-plan/task-bookkeeping updates as `14a86e3` with the required task-finished commit message and pushed `master` to origin.
- Final verified gates for this task were `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`; the task markdown now reads `<status>completed</status> <passes>true</passes>`, and `.ralph/task_switch.sh` already advanced Ralph bookkeeping.
- If I had to quit immediately now, there is no remaining work for task 03 in this turn; the next turn should read the new current Ralph task and continue from there.

- Refreshed `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, read the choose-task rules, and checked the repo shape to avoid assuming the stack; the active codebase is a Rust workspace (`crates/runner`, `crates/setup-sql`, `crates/operator-log`) plus the Go `cockroachdb_molt/molt` verify-service slice.
- Confirmed there are zero active failing bug tasks, zero active blocker tags in `.ralph/current_tasks.md`, and zero meta-tasks eligible ahead of failing work. Among the remaining failing tasks, docs should wait for stable operator-facing CLI/config surfaces.
- Wrote `.ralph/current_task.txt` to `.ralph/tasks/story-02-verify-service-ergonomics/task-06-standardize-tls-config-patterns.md` because TLS shape is still split across Rust runner config and Go verify-service config, and standardizing that contract is the highest-leverage unblocked code task before the docs story. I also noted that `verify-service run` already exists in the Go CLI, so task 05 looked less urgent from the current code state.
- If I have to quit now, the next turn should execute task 06 with the `tdd` skill, then run the required gates: `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint`.

- Read previous progress, `.ralph/current_task.txt`, and the required `tdd` / `improve-code-boundaries` skills.
- Confirmed the active task is `.ralph/tasks/story-02-verify-service-ergonomics/task-06-standardize-tls-config-patterns.md`; next I am checking whether it is still in planning (`TO BE VERIFIED`) or ready to execute (`NOW EXECUTE`).
- If I have to quit now, the next turn should read the task file and any plan file under its `_plans` directory, then either write the required plan and stop or execute that approved plan.

- Inspected the active task file and confirmed there is no existing plan file yet, so this turn must produce a plan and stop.
- Read the main TLS contract surfaces: `crates/runner/src/config/{parser,mod}.rs`, `cockroachdb_molt/molt/verifyservice/{config.go,config_test.go}`, `README.md`, and runner README contract tests.
- Current understanding: runner webhook TLS already matches verify listener TLS except for optional mTLS CA naming; runner destination decomposed TLS already uses `ca_cert_path` / `client_cert_path` / `client_key_path`; verify still flattens DB TLS beside `url`, and docs/tests need a single correspondence story.
- If I have to quit now, the next turn should finish the planning notes with concrete red-green slices, create `.ralph/tasks/story-02-verify-service-ergonomics/task-06-standardize-tls-config-patterns_plans/...md`, end it with `TO BE VERIFIED`, and then quit immediately.

- Read the active task `.ralph/tasks/story-02-verify-service-ergonomics/task-06-standardize-tls-config-patterns.md` and confirmed there was no existing plan artifact, so this turn stayed in planning mode only.
- Inspected the relevant Rust and Go TLS config/runtime/doc boundaries: runner parser/config/runtime/runtime-plan/tests, verify-service config/tests/CLI/tests, README, and operator/image contract tests.
- Wrote the required plan file at `.ralph/tasks/story-02-verify-service-ergonomics/task-06-standardize-tls-config-patterns_plans/2026-04-25-standardize-tls-config-patterns-plan.md`.
- The plan uses both required skills (`tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries`) and ends with `TO BE VERIFIED`.
- Key planned contract decision: keep verify DB `sslmode` in the URL, move verify DB cert paths under nested `verify.{source,destination}.tls.*`, and add optional `webhook.tls.client_ca_path` to runner so listener TLS vocabulary matches verify.
- Key boundary cleanup planned: stop mixing verify DB URL ownership and TLS path ownership in one flat DTO; use one honest nested TLS-material boundary instead.
- I did not execute code changes, run tests, or modify the task status because the task instructions require quitting immediately after creating the first plan.
- If I have to quit now, the next turn should read this plan file, review the proposed nested verify DB TLS shape and runner webhook mTLS symmetry, and either keep it as `TO BE VERIFIED` if the design still needs adjustment or replace the tail with `NOW EXECUTE` only once the design is considered correct.

- Read previous progress, `.ralph/current_task.txt`, and the required `tdd` / `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions again at startup.
- Confirmed the active task remains `.ralph/tasks/story-02-verify-service-ergonomics/task-06-standardize-tls-config-patterns.md`; next step is reading the task file plus the existing plan file to determine whether it should stay `TO BE VERIFIED` or be flipped to `NOW EXECUTE`.
- If I have to quit immediately now, the next turn should inspect the task and plan contents, then either update the plan tail to `NOW EXECUTE` and stop or keep it in verification if the interface design is still wrong.

- Read the current task file and the existing task-06 plan. The plan still ends in `TO BE VERIFIED`, so this turn must either keep it there or promote it to `NOW EXECUTE` and quit immediately.
- Validated the current code shape: verify listener already has `client_ca_path`; verify database config is still flat (`ca_cert_path` beside `url`); runner destination already uses nested `tls.{ca_cert_path,client_cert_path,client_key_path}`; runner webhook runtime still hardcodes `with_no_client_auth()`.
- Next I am checking runner runtime-plan / config types and operator-surface tests so I can decide whether the proposed nested verify DB TLS boundary plus runner webhook mTLS parity is stable enough to execute.

- Re-read the task, the existing task-06 plan, and the relevant runner/verify TLS config/runtime/test surfaces to verify whether the proposed design was execution-ready.
- Confirmed the boundary decision is sound against the current code: verify listener already uses `client_ca_path`, verify database config is the remaining flat boundary to clean up, runner destination already uses the target TLS field names, and runner webhook still needs real mTLS runtime support.
- Updated `.ralph/tasks/story-02-verify-service-ergonomics/task-06-standardize-tls-config-patterns_plans/2026-04-25-standardize-tls-config-patterns-plan.md` to end with `NOW EXECUTE`.
- If I have to quit now, the next turn should read the plan, execute the TDD slices in order, keep the no-backward-compatibility stance, run `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`, and only then update task bookkeeping.

- Confirmed task-06 is in execution mode: the approved plan ends with NOW EXECUTE.
- Read the active task, the plan, and the current verify/runner TLS config surfaces. Verify still has flat DB TLS fields in `cockroachdb_molt/molt/verifyservice/config.go`; runner already uses nested destination TLS but webhook TLS lacks `client_ca_path` runtime support.
- Next I am starting the first TDD slice on verify: update the public config tests/fixtures to require nested `verify.{source,destination}.tls.*`, make the old flat shape reject loudly, then run the targeted Go tests before moving to runner.

- Executed the verify-side TDD slices: changed verify DB config from flat TLS fields to nested `verify.{source,destination}.tls.*`, updated all verify config testdata, patched `config.go`, `config_test.go`, `verify_runner_test.go`, and CLI runtime config generation, and verified `go test ./verifyservice` plus `go test ./cmd/verifyservice` pass.
- Executed the runner listener TDD slices: added `webhook.tls.client_ca_path`, propagated it through config parsing, runtime planning, validated config output (`https+mtls` plus full TLS material label), and implemented rustls client verification from the configured CA bundle.
- Fixed a real boundary/runtime issue discovered by the new mtls test: a rejected TLS handshake used to crash the runner; now it emits a `webhook.tls_handshake_failed` error event and keeps serving so valid clients can still connect.
- Updated runner test support that derived URL schemes from the webhook mode string so `https+mtls` still maps to an HTTPS base URL.
- Updated README and verify image harness toward the new docs/runtime contract: runner docs now mention `webhook.tls.client_ca_path`, verify docs now show nested DB `tls` blocks, and the README has the new side-by-side TLS mapping table.
- Next I need to run the focused Rust test sets (`config_contract`, `bootstrap_contract`, `readme_operator_surface_contract`, and any verify-image/readme contract affected by the harness/docs changes), then run the required top-level lanes `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`, and only after that update task bookkeeping.

- Finished the implementation and verification for task-06. `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed; I did not run `make test-long`.
- Final shipped behavior: verify database cert material now lives under nested `verify.{source,destination}.tls.*`; runner webhook now supports optional `webhook.tls.client_ca_path`, reports `https+mtls`, and enforces client certificates at runtime without crashing the process on rejected handshakes.
- Updated README/operator docs and harnesses so runner/verify TLS mapping is explicit and the verify examples use the same nested TLS vocabulary.
- Updated the task markdown to `<status>completed</status> <passes>true</passes>` and checked the acceptance boxes, including the greenfield decision to remove obsolete flat verify DB TLS fields instead of aliasing them.
- Next immediate steps are Ralph bookkeeping only: run `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, commit everything including `.ralph`, push, and then stop.
